text:
Once upon a time, in the heart of a lush, enchanted forest, there was a small, yet magnificent oak tree named Oakley. Oakley was not just any ordinary oak; it was a tree with a special gift—the ability to communicate with all the creatures that lived around it. This gift made Oakley the guardian of the forest, a trusted friend to all.

One crisp autumn morning, as the leaves danced in the breeze and the sun cast golden rays through the canopy, a young squirrel named Swift found herself lost in the vastness of the forest. She had been playing near the edge of the forest and wandered too far from home. The dense underbrush and the maze-like paths seemed endless, and she felt overwhelmed with fear.

Oakley, sensing the distress in the air, reached out its branches towards Swift. With a gentle nudge, a leaf from Oakley's bough landed softly on Swift’s nose. She sniffed it, and suddenly, the way ahead became clear
